pages.title: Asclepius — A Trust Substrate for Clinical AI
pages.description: Open, vendor-neutral C++ runtime that wraps any clinical AI with audit-grade safety: signed Merkle ledger, policy chain, drift, consent, evidence bundles.
headings: Q1 2026 alone cost American health systems more than the entire clinical-AI tooling market earned.
Sharp HealthCare AI-scribe encounters recorded without patient consent.
UnitedHealth nH Predict denials reportedly reversed on appeal.
of healthcare executives cite "lack of guidelines" and "resource allocation" as the primary AI-adoption barriers.
Five primitives. One library. One library binding into every clinical AI you ship.
A single call traces the same six edges, every time.
Fifteen lines wraps any model in audit-grade safety.
A regulator should be able to verify your bundle on a laptop, offline, in under a second.
A trust substrate that costs more than the model it wraps would not be a substrate. Numbers we will defend.
Every adjacent tool solves a fragment. Asclepius is what you'd get if you built the missing whole.
The questions a CTO asks before putting it in front of the AI Governance Office.
Liability is biting now, not someday.
A trust substrate must be auditable to be trusted.

pages.title: Integrations — Asclepius
pages.description: Copy-paste recipes for wrapping clinical-AI tools in Asclepius. Ambient scribe, in-house model, agent, FHIR sidecar, batch eval. C++ and Python side-by-side.
headings: resulting bundle (USTAR tar — opens with stock tar)
The pattern is always the same.

pages.title: Research — Asclepius
pages.description: Annotated bibliography of papers Asclepius is built on. Merkle 1979, Ed25519, BLAKE2, drift literature, FHIR R5, clinical-AI accountability canon.
headings: Crypto we chose to build on.
The Merkle tree — log integrity for forty-five years.
Ed25519 — the signature scheme we sign with.
BLAKE2 — the hash the ledger leans on.
libsodium — the implementation we trust to be right.
SLSA — the L1/L2/L3 ladder we mirror.
EMD — ordinal drift, where order matters.
Model cards — declared once, bound to every inference.
Do no harm — the operational imperative.
The accountability gap we are filling.
JSON Schema — the entry shape contract.
